overview: This bird is a Rufous-capped Warbler (Basileuterus rufifrons), identified by its distinct rusty-red cap and white eyebrow stripe. National Audubon Society +1 They are typically found in Mexico and Central America, but are rare visitors to brushy canyons in southeastern Arizona and southern Texas. Wikipedia +1 These warblers have a bright yellow throat and breast, contrasting with a grayish-olive back, and often cock their long tails vertically.
